name: Alfredo-Sauced Chicken and Rice
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups cooked regular long grain rice
1/2 teaspoon dried tarragon or marjoram leaves
6 boneless skinless chicken breast halves (about 1 1/2 lb)
1/3 cup roasted red bell pepper, sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a greased rectangle baking dish, mix the cooked wild rice, cooked regular long grain rice, 3/4 cup of Alfredo sauce, 1/4 teaspoon tarragon, and 1/8 teaspoon pepper.
3. Spray a 12-inch nonstick skillet with cooking spray and heat over medium-high heat.
4. Sprinkle seasoned salt on the chicken breasts.
5. Cook the chicken for 3-4 minutes, turning once, until browned.
6. Place the cooked chicken on top of the rice mixture.
7. Mix the remaining Alfredo sauce, tarragon, asparagus, mushrooms, and bell pepper in a bowl.
8. Spoon the mixture over the chicken and rice.
9. Cover the dish and bake for 1 hour or until the juice from the thickest pieces of chicken is no longer pink when cut.
Makes 6 servings.
Per serving: 535 calories, 280 total fat, 31 grams fat, 150 mg cholesterol, 485 mg sodium, 30 grams carbohydrates, 36 grams protein
Diet Exchanges: 2 starch, 5 very lean meat, 4 fat
Betty Crocker New Chicken Cookbook #141, p. 86
